step.title: Create Scripts
step.goal: Generate script files based on the designs from Step 2. Each script gets PEP 723 header, docstring, and function stubs.
step.note:
Scripts are FUNDAMENTAL to skills. This step creates the actual Python files with proper structure so they can be filled in.
Scripts use {base_dir} convention - when the skill runs, it knows its installation location and can reference scripts accordingly.

substep.title: Review Script Designs
substep.action:
Review the script designs from Step 2:
For each script, confirm: - name: filename (kebab-case.py) - purpose: what it does - inputs: command-line arguments - outputs: what it returns/creates - dependencies: Python packages

check.ask: Some script designs are missing details. Let's complete them: - What arguments does {{script_name}} take? - What does it output? - What packages does it need?

substep.title: Generate Script Files
Review Generated Scripts

section.title: PEP 723 Script Format
section.description: All scripts use PEP 723 inline metadata for dependencies.
section.template:
```python # /// script # requires-python = ">=3.11" # dependencies = [ # "pyyaml>=6.0", # "requests>=2.28", # ] # /// """ Brief description of what this script does.
Usage: uv run script.py <required_arg> [optional_arg] Output: Description of what it produces/returns """
import sys from pathlib import Path
def main() -> None: # Implementation pass
if __name__ == "__main__": main() ```

section.title: Script Execution
section.description: Scripts are executed via uv run, which handles dependencies automatically.

rules.rule: The {base_dir} placeholder resolves to skill installation path
Dependencies are installed automatically on first run

section.example:
# In SKILL.md or step files: <action type="script">uv run {base_dir}/scripts/validate.py {{input_file}}</action>
# Or via workflow engine: <action type="script">uv run scripts/scaffold.py {{name}}</action>

pattern.name: Scaffold Script
pattern.purpose: Creates directory structure and template files

section.title: Error Handling

rules.rule: Exit code 0 for success, 1 for errors
Print errors to stderr: print("Error: ...", file=sys.stderr)
Validate inputs early, fail fast with clear messages

section.title: Output Conventions

rules.rule: Error messages: print to stderr with "Error:" prefix
Structured data: Use JSON for machine-readable output
Status indicators: Use emoji (checkmark, x, warning) for human readability

section.example:
# Success print(f"Created: {output_path}")
# Validation result print(f"Validation PASSED: {file_path}") print(f"Validation FAILED: {error_count} error(s)")
# Structured output import json print(json.dumps({"status": "success", "files_created": [...]})

example.name: Scaffold Script
example.context: Creates directory structure for a new agent
example.config:
```python # /// script # requires-python = ">=3.11" # dependencies = ["pyyaml>=6.0"] # /// """ Create directory structure and template files for a new agent.
Usage: uv run scaffold-agent.py <agent_id> Output: Directory created with agent-config.yaml template """
import sys from pathlib import Path
TEMPLATE = """# Agent: {agent_id} agent_id: {agent_id} # TODO: Fill in remaining fields """
def scaffold_agent(agent_id: str) -> None: # Validation if not agent_id.islower(): print(f"Error: agent_id must be lowercase", file=sys.stderr) sys.exit(1)
# Create directory agent_dir = Path(f"vera-source/agents/{agent_id}") agent_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
# Write template config_file = agent_dir / "agent-config.yaml" config_file.write_text(TEMPLATE.format(agent_id=agent_id))
print(f"Created: {config_file}")
def main() -> None: if len(sys.argv) != 2: print("Usage: uv run scaffold-agent.py <agent_id>") sys.exit(1) scaffold_agent(sys.argv[1])
if __name__ == "__main__": main() ```
example.rationale: Standard scaffold pattern: validate input, create structure, write template, report success. Uses sys.exit(1) for errors, prints to stderr.

example.name: Validation Script
example.context: Validates a skill directory for completeness
example.config:
```python # /// script # requires-python = ">=3.11" # dependencies = ["pyyaml>=6.0"] # /// """ Validate a skill directory for correctness and completeness.
Usage: uv run validate-skill.py <skill_dir> Output: Validation result with errors/warnings """
import sys from pathlib import Path import yaml
def validate_skill(skill_dir: Path) -> list[str]: errors = []
# Check required files config = skill_dir / "skill-config.yaml" if not config.exists(): errors.append("Missing: skill-config.yaml")
# ... more checks
return errors
def main() -> None: if len(sys.argv) != 2: print("Usage: uv run validate-skill.py <skill_dir>") sys.exit(1)
skill_dir = Path(sys.argv[1]) errors = validate_skill(skill_dir)
if errors: for e in errors: print(f" {e}") print(f"Validation FAILED: {len(errors)} error(s)") sys.exit(1) else: print(f"Validation PASSED: {skill_dir}")
if __name__ == "__main__": main() ```
example.rationale: Standard validation pattern: collect errors, report all at once, exit with appropriate code. Clear output format.
